g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about Agile Tool Sets - the essential software that powers modern software development.
g.text: A Tool Set for Agile Process

g.data-narration: An Agile tool set is a collection of software tools that support Agile methodology, including project management, version control, and testing tools.
g.text: What is an Agile Tool Set?
A comprehensive collection of software tools that support Agile methodology implementation
including project management, version control, CI/CD, testing, and collaboration tools.

g.data-narration: Agile tools enable teams to visualize work, manage backlogs, facilitate communication, and maintain rapid iteration cycles for continuous delivery.
g.text: Why Do Tools Matter in Agile?
• Enable visualization of work and tracking progress
• Automate build, test, and deployment processes

g.data-narration: Project management tools like Jira, Trello, and Azure DevOps help teams organize work items, manage backlogs, plan sprints, and track progress.
Git is the de facto standard for version control, enabling parallel development with branches. GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket provide cloud platforms for collaboration.
CI/CD tools automate build, test, and deployment processes. Jenkins leads with extensibility, while CircleCI and Azure Pipelines offer cloud-based solutions.
Testing tools ensure quality through automation: Selenium for browser testing, JUnit for unit tests, Postman for APIs, and Cypress for modern end-to-end testing.
Collaboration tools like Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Confluence enable real-time communication and documentation essential for distributed Agile teams.
SonarQube provides code quality analysis, while Docker and Kubernetes enable containerization for consistent deployments across development, testing, and production environments.
A complete Agile tool chain flows from project management through version control, CI/CD, testing, and deployment - each tool integrated with the next.
In sprint tracking, burndown charts visualize remaining work over time. Jira helps teams monitor progress and adjust commitments based on actual velocity.
g.text: Example: Sprint Tracking with Burndown Chart
Day 1-3: 20 pts • Day 4-6: +15 pts • Bug: +10 pts (Day 7)

g.data-narration: A Jenkins pipeline automates the software delivery process through stages: Checkout, Install, Test, Build, and Deploy - all defined in a Jenkinsfile.
Key takeaways: Classify tools into categories, understand Git vs GitHub, remember CI/CD pipeline stages, and prioritize tool integration for effective Agile workflows.
g.text: Key Takeaways: Don't confuse Git (VCS) with GitHub (Platform) • Automated testing essential • Tool integration is critical
